title: Silique RNA Extraction
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"e2b8p","text":"This protocol was based on a 2010 paper by Meng and Feldman for extraction of RNA from arabidopsis siliques. While the protocol remains unchanged, I have made some slight edits to streamline it in the lab and also added some notes regarding my results applying this protocol to tobacoo fruit tissue.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"eiqrg","text":"All solutions should be made with DEPC-treated water and all work should be completed on an RNase-free work surface with RNase-free tools","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"632hm","text":"Make up the extraction buffer:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9ppt7","text":"100mM Tris-HCl, pH 9.5","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ep7f2","text":"150mM NaCl","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5qadg","text":"1.0% sarkosyl","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"uu6n","text":"5mM DTT (or 0.5% β-ME)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"v8n","text":"Make up ~3.2mL of 75% ethanol (RNase-free) and at least 200µL 3M sodium acetate (pH 5.2) for each sample to be extracted.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ct86k","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e0odp","text":"Set a centrifuge to 4ºC and allow to cool while setting up.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"clkdl","text":"Many components of this extraction are hazardous (phenol, chloroform, TRIzol) and should only be used in a fume hood. Also, make sure to properly discard of consumables that come in contact with these componenets.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Add three stainless steel BBs to each 2-mL tube with tissue in liquid nitrogen and homogenize the samples in a Retsch mixer mill for 60 seconds at 25 Hz. (Retsch MM400, 20.745.0001, https://s3.amazonaws.com/pr-journal/xk9jive.png )
After brief mixing, vortex vigorously for 5 min, and spin for 5 min at 11 000×g. 00:05:00, Vortex 00:05:00, Spin at 11,000g
Transfer the supernatant to a new 2-mL Eppendorf tube, add 0.5 volume of chloroform (~500μL) and vortex for 2 min. undetermined, Chloroform 00:02:00, Vortex
Add 0.5 volume of acid phenol (water-saturated; ~500μL) and vortex for another 2 min. undetermined, Acid Phenol 00:02:00, Vortex
Centrifuge at 11,000g for 15 min. 00:15:00, Spin at 11,000g

Carefully remove the upper aqueous phase (avoiding the interface) to a new RNase-free 2-mL Eppendorf tube, and add 90μL 3 M sodium acetate (pH 5.2) and 600μL isopropanol. Mix briefly. undetermined, 3M Sodium Acetate undetermined, Isopropanol
Incubate at room temp for 10 min then spin at 11,000g for 10 min. 00:10:00, Incubation 00:10:00, Spin at 11,000g
Discard the upper aqueous phase and wash the pellet with 1 mL 75% ethanol (RNase-free). undetermined, 75% Ethanol
Spin at 11,000g for 10 min, and discard the supernatant. Air dry the pellet briefly (<5 min). 00:10:00, Spin at 11,000g 00:05:00, Air dry
Resuspend the pellet in1 mL TRIzol reagent and vortex until dissolved. undetermined, TRIzol
Add 200µL chloroform and mix by shaking vigorously for 15s. undetermined, Chloroform 00:00:15, Vortex
Incubate for 2–3 min, then centrifuge at 11,000g at 4°C for 15 min. 00:02:00, Incubation 00:15:00, Spin at 11,000g and 4ºC

Transfer the upper aqueous phase to a new well-labeled RNase-free 1.5-mL Eppendorf tube containing 500μL isopropanol. Mix, then incubate for 10 min. This is the tube that will be used for final storage. A white mat may form in the aqueous phase, and should also be transferred. undetermined, Isopropanol 00:10:00, Incubation
Spin at 11 000×g at 4°C for 15 min 00:15:00, Spin at 11,000g and 4ºC
Discard the supernatant and dry the pellet.
Dissolve the RNA pellet in 100μL DEPC-treated, sterilized ddH2O. undetermined, RNase-free Water
Add 10μL DEPC-treated 3 M sodium acetate (pH 5.2) and 250μL RNase-free 100% ethanol and mix. undetermined, 3M Sodium Acetate undetermined, Absolute Ethanol
Incubate for 20 min, then spin at 11,000g at 4°C for 15 min. 00:20:00, Incubation 00:15:00, Spin at 11,000g and 4ºC
Wash the pellet with 1.2 mL 75% ethanol, then spin at 11,000g at 4°C for 10 min. undetermined, 75% Ethanol 00:10:00, Spin at 11,000g and 4ºC
Discard the supernatant and dry the RNA pellet.
Dissolve the pellet in 50μL DEPC-treated, sterilized ddH2O, and store at – 80°C. undetermined, RNase-free Water
Add 1.2mL of Extraction Buffer (with βME or DTT added) to the frozen tissue and mix. undetermined, Extraction buffer
Wash the pellet with 1.2 mL 75% ethanol RNase-free), and spin at 11 000×g at 4°C for 10 min. undetermined, 75% Ethanol 00:10:00, Spin at 11,000g and 4ºC
creator: Alex Rajewski
